questions.original_question: 路特斯科技的主营业务是什么？
questions.answer:
Lotus Technology 的主要业务包括设计、开发和销售以“Lotus”品牌命名的豪华电池电动汽车（BEV生活方式车型），以及销售以“Lotus”品牌命名的豪华跑车。此外，Lotus Technology 还提供汽车零部件和周边产品，并通过为OEM客户提供汽车设计和开发服务、技术相关服务及售后服务来生成收入。Lotus Technology 也从事汽车及其关键部件的研发活动，以及汽车工程开发服务。

以上信息基于截至2024年9月19日的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2023, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology had 3,180 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 56.7% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$750.3 million, US\$724.6 million, and US\$110.5 million in 2023, 2022, and 2021, respectively.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

rag_info.chunk_content:
British Formula 1 constructors were caught out by the decision of the sport’s governing body to limit engine size to just 1.5 litres for the 1961 season. None could quite believe that it would actually go ahead with a limit which equated to what had been Formula 2 specification the previous year. The main British engine supplier to Formula 1, Coventry Climax, was unprepared for the move, and so the major teams (Lotus, Cooper, and even BRM) were forced to rely on the outdated four-cylinder FPF engine. Ferrari, by contrast, had been working on a new and far superior V6 power unit for some time.

Coventry Climax hastily decided to develop a new V8 engine and issued installation drawings to the main teams. The eventual FWMV engine was in fact deeper and longer than planned, forcing chassis redesigns for those teams which had intentions to use the unit. For 1961, this particular problem did not affect Lotus. Colin Chapman’s personal relationship with Coventry Climax’s Leonard Lee was at an all-time low following rows over the quality of the FWE engines being supplied for the Elite road car. Although he designed the Type 21 to use the projected new V8 engine, Chapman

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. Operating Results 
summary: We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer focused on creating premium lifestyle vehicles for everyday use under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." Our portfolio includes a variety of Lotus-branded cars that reflect luxury and sophistication. Since our establishment, we have achieved notable success, delivering 6,970 vehicles in 2023, which encompassed both lifestyle and sports models. By the year's end, our global workforce reached 3,180 full-time employees, with around 56.7% dedicated to research and development across teams in China and Europe. Our total revenues for 2023 soared to $679.0 million, a remarkable rise from $9.6 million in 2022. However, despite this growth, we faced a net loss of $750.3 million in 2023, an increase from losses of $724.6 million in 2022 and $110.5 million in 2021.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Services. Lotus Technology generates revenues mainly by providing automotive design and development services to OEM customers, technology-related services, and after-sales service.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Revenues 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Revenues**

The company generates its revenues primarily through the sale of goods and services, with a strong emphasis on automotive products and design services. The key revenue sources are categorized into sales of goods and service revenues.

**Sales of Goods**: 
- The company's sales of goods include battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, Lotus-brand sports cars, auto parts, and peripheral products. 
- For the six months ended June 30, 2024, sales of goods totaled $382,893, representing 96.2% of total revenues, a slight increase from $124,854 (96.0%) during the same period in 2023. 
- For the year ended December 31, 2023, sales of goods reached $660,158, accounting for 97.2% of total revenues, a significant decrease from $1,186 (12.4%) in 2022.

**Service Revenues**: 
- The company also derives revenue from automotive design and development services, technology-related services, and after-sales support. 
- For the six months ended June 30, 2024, service revenues were $15,222, making up 3.8% of total revenues, an increase from $5,181 (4.0%) in 2023. 
- For the year ended December 31, 2023, service revenues amounted to $18,850, which constituted 2.8% of total revenues, compared to $8,371 (87.6%) in 2022.

**Total Revenues**: 
- The total revenues for the six months ended June 30, 2024, were $398,115, a significant rise from $130,035 in 2023. 
- For the year ended December 31, 2023, total revenues stood at $679,008, a substantial increase from $9,557 in 2022.

**Costs Associated with Revenues**: 
- The company incurs costs categorized into cost of goods sold (COGS) and cost of services. 
- For the six months ended June 30, 2024, the cost of goods sold was $340,882, representing 98.2% of total revenue costs. 
- For the year ended December 31, 2023, COGS totaled $564,741, accounting for 97.9% of total revenue costs. 
- The cost of services for the six months ended June 30, 2024, was $6,321, which is 1.8% of total revenue costs. 
- For the year ended December 31, 2023, the cost of services reached $12,086, representing 2.1% of total revenue costs.

Overall, the company’s revenue generation strategy highlights a robust focus on product distribution and service delivery, with notable shifts in revenue composition and cost structure over the analyzed periods. All financial figures are reported in thousands of US dollars.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, specializing in the design and development of luxury lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich racing heritage spanning over 70 years, Lotus is renowned for its high-performance standards in both design and engineering. Since Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK in 2017, we have successfully transitioned into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, benefiting from Geely's support across various operational areas.

Our flagship model, the Eletre, is a luxury E-segment SUV that showcases our innovative 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R stands out with an impressive 905 horsepower, achieving acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds, and offers a WLTP range of 490 km, complemented by rapid charging capabilities. Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in 2023, and we launched our second model, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries set to begin in March 2024. Furthermore, we have plans to introduce two additional models in the near future.

Our research and development capabilities, deeply rooted in Lotus's sports car legacy, serve as a significant competitive advantage, exemplified by the five-year development of the EPA, which facilitates rapid model rollout and enhances production efficiency. Additionally, we have developed an advanced Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) featuring Level 4-ready hardware and a deployable LiDAR system.

To enhance customer experience, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores in high-traffic areas alongside a global digital platform for vehicle customization and purchasing. As of June 30, 2024, our global distribution network comprises 207 stores. In 2023, we delivered a total of 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024. Our workforce consists of 2,989 full-time employees, with a substantial 54.6% dedicated to research and development. 

This comprehensive approach positions us at the forefront of the luxury electric vehicle market, driving innovation and excellence in mobility solutions.

Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

title: Revenues 
summary: The company primarily generates revenue through the sale of goods and services, with a significant focus on automotive products. Key revenue streams include a diverse range of Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models that align with modern consumer preferences for sustainability and innovative design. Additionally, the company collaborates with Lotus UK to develop high-performance sports cars, reinforcing its commitment to engineering excellence and advanced technology. The portfolio also features durable auto parts that support both BEV models and other automotive applications, as well as peripheral products designed to enhance the overall automotive experience.

A detailed analysis of revenue sources reveals a dramatic shift in 2023, where sales of goods surged to US$660,158, representing 97.2% of total revenues, while services revenues contributed only US$18,850 (2.8%). This marks a significant change from 2022, when sales of goods were merely US$1,186 (12.4%) and services revenues dominated at US$8,371 (87.6%). In 2021, the trend continued with sales of goods at US$369 (10.0%) and services at US$3,318 (90.0%). Overall, total revenues increased to US$679,008 in 2023, compared to US$9,557 in 2022 and US$3,687 in 2021. This evolution underscores the company’s strategic focus on product sales and its impact on revenue composition over the years, with all figures expressed in thousands of US dollars.

title: Our ability to maintain strategic partnership with Geely Holding 
summary: Our strategic partnership with Geely Holding is a pivotal element of our business model, providing us with a competitive advantage through rapid commercialization and an asset-light operating framework. This partnership minimizes our need for substantial upfront capital expenditures compared to traditional Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs), allowing us to allocate resources more efficiently.

We have formalized our collaboration through various agreements, which include technology licensing, manufacturing cooperation, and supply frameworks. These agreements not only bolster our operational capabilities but also enhance our ability to manage supply chain risks effectively. By leveraging Geely's established infrastructure and expertise, we can accelerate product development timelines, ensuring that we remain agile in a rapidly evolving market.

This partnership is not just a strategic alliance but a comprehensive integration of resources and knowledge, which positions us favorably against competitors. As we continue to navigate the complexities of the automotive industry, our relationship with Geely will be instrumental in driving innovation and maintaining our market presence. 

Overall, our collaboration with Geely Holding is a cornerstone of our strategy, enabling us to pursue growth opportunities while maintaining financial prudence and operational efficiency.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA) has received approval from its board of directors for a Merger Agreement involving Lotus Technology Inc. (LTC) and its subsidiaries, Lotus Temp Limited and Lotus EV Limited. The agreement, which outlines a two-step merger process, was initially dated January 31, 2023, and amended on October 11, 2023. In the first step, Lotus Temp Limited will merge with LCAA, making LCAA a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC, and converting its shareholders into LTC shareholders. The second step involves the surviving entity from the first merger merging into Lotus EV Limited, which will also become a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTC. 

Shareholders of LCAA will vote on the proposed Business Combination, which, if approved, will transition their shares to LTC shares. Key actions prior to the merger include the conversion of LTC's preferred shares into ordinary shares, the adoption of amended articles of association, and a recapitalization process that will affect the share structure and outstanding options. 

LTC, a Cayman Islands holding company, has undergone a restructuring to eliminate its previous variable interest entity (VIE) structure, now directly operating through its subsidiaries in China. This change presents certain risks, including regulatory challenges in China and potential trading prohibitions in the U.S. under the Holding Foreign Companies Accountable Act (HFCAA). The company plans to list its American Depositary Shares (ADSs) on Nasdaq following the merger.

The document also outlines the financial performance of LTC, including audited and unaudited financial statements, and details the registration fees associated with the securities being registered in connection with the merger. The extraordinary general meeting for LCAA shareholders will address the merger proposals, with the board recommending approval. However, shareholders are advised to consider potential conflicts of interest among LCAA's directors and officers regarding the Business Combination. The SEC has not evaluated the merits of the transactions, and any claims to the contrary are considered criminal offenses.

With the production of the Elite delayed, the Seven took on an unexpected mantle as Lotus' main road car effort, and about 60 cars were built during 1958. The increasing workload of the Lotus empire at this time led the company to move from its Hornsey base to a much larger factory not far away in Cheshunt. Another thing to be said of the Seven was that it brought in the funds that Lotus needed to get the Elite into proper production. The Seven itself was also a costly car to make, taking many man-hours to put together what was essentially just a few pieces of metal. The chassis, in particular, sub-contracted to the Progress Chassis Co., was a somewhat expensive item. 

1957 Series 1 Seven Milestone Car:

- Initial Acceleration: Exceptional
- Running Economy: Efficient
- Handling: Superior
- Dry Weather Grip: Excellent
- Motoring Fun: Sheer

"What other machine can offer such initial acceleration, running economy, handling, dry weather grip, and sheer motoring fun?" - John Miles Milestone Car

Tasman Car Type 32B. Although he was forced to retire (his only retirement in the series) in the first main event race of the series (the New Zealand Grand Prix) when he was rammed by Bruce McLaren’s Cooper, he went on to win nine of the total of thirteen races (and five of the main eight events). Taking a second, a fifth, and an eighth place in addition, Jimmy had clinched the Championship (ahead of Bruce McLaren and Jack Brabham) by the sixth race of the series. An extraordinarily good result for a single driver/car entry in this highly competitive series. The Type 32B is unique in Lotus history in that it was designed and built specifically for Jim Clark, and only he drove it for all thirteen of its races with Team Lotus. And what’s more, it won nearly seventy-five percent of the races it competed in. As happened with many of the Team Lotus Tasman cars over the years, and ostensibly to save the cost of shipping the car back to the UK, at the end of the 1965 series, the 32B was sold off to a local racer. In the case of the 32B, this was

British Formula 1 constructors were caught out by the decision of the sport’s governing body to limit engine size to just 1.5 litres for the 1961 season. None could quite believe that it would actually go ahead with a limit which equated to what had been Formula 2 specification the previous year. The main British engine supplier to Formula 1, Coventry Climax, was unprepared for the move, and so the major teams (Lotus, Cooper, and even BRM) were forced to rely on the outdated four-cylinder FPF engine. Ferrari, by contrast, had been working on a new and far superior V6 power unit for some time.

Coventry Climax hastily decided to develop a new V8 engine and issued installation drawings to the main teams. The eventual FWMV engine was in fact deeper and longer than planned, forcing chassis redesigns for those teams which had intentions to use the unit. For 1961, this particular problem did not affect Lotus. Colin Chapman’s personal relationship with Coventry Climax’s Leonard Lee was at an all-time low following rows over the quality of the FWE engines being supplied for the Elite road car. Although he designed the Type 21 to use the projected new V8 engine, Chapman

The board of directors of Lotus Technology Corporation will determine whether, to what extent, at what times and places, and under what conditions or regulations the accounts and books of Lotus Technology Corporation will be open to the inspection by Lotus Technology Corporation shareholders, and no Lotus Technology Corporation shareholder will otherwise have any right of inspecting any account or book or document of Lotus Technology Corporation except as required by law or authorized by the board of directors of Lotus Technology Corporation or Lotus Technology Corporation shareholders by special resolution.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Inspection of Books and Records 
summary: The board of directors of LTC holds the power to determine the conditions for shareholder access to the company's accounts and books. Shareholders do not possess an automatic right to inspect these documents; such access is contingent upon board approval, legal requirements, or a special resolution passed by the shareholders.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Inspection of Books and Records 
summary: The board of directors will establish the guidelines for shareholder access to the company's accounts and records. Shareholders may only inspect these documents if granted permission by the board, mandated by law, or approved through a special resolution by the shareholders.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology will need significant capital to, among other things, conduct research and development, expand its production capacity, and roll out its sales network, as well as delivery and servicing centers. As Lotus Technology ramps up its production capacity and operations, it may also require significant capital to maintain its property, plant, and equipment, and such costs may be greater than what the company currently anticipates. Lotus Technology expects that its level of capital expenditures will be significantly affected by consumer demand for its products and services. The fact that Lotus Technology has a limited operating history means it has limited historical data on the demand for its products and services. As a result, the company's future capital requirements may be uncertain, and actual capital requirements may be significantly different from what Lotus Technology currently anticipates. Lotus Technology may need to seek equity or debt financing to finance a portion of its capital expenditures. Such financing might not be available to the company in a timely manner or on terms that are acceptable, or at all. If Lotus Technology cannot obtain sufficient capital on acceptable terms, its business, financial condition, and prospects may be materially and adversely affected. Lotus Technology's ability to obtain the necessary financing to carry out its business plan is subject to a number of factors, including general market conditions and investor acceptance of its business plan. These factors may make the timing, amount, terms, and conditions of such financing unattractive or unavailable to Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our business plans require a significant amount of capital. In addition, our future capital needs may require us to obtain additional equity or debt financing that may dilute our shareholders or introduce covenants that may restrict our operations or our ability to pay dividends. 
summary: The company is facing significant capital requirements for research and development, production expansion, and establishing a sales and service network. As production increases, the need for additional capital to maintain property and equipment may arise, influenced by unpredictable consumer demand due to the company's limited operational history. This uncertainty could lead to future capital needs that diverge from current estimates, necessitating potential equity or debt financing. However, securing such financing may be challenging due to market conditions and investor interest, which could result in unfavorable terms or timing. A failure to raise sufficient funds could force the company to cut spending, delay investments, or alter its structure, jeopardizing operations and potentially leading to a cessation of activities.

Additionally, the company has entered into put option agreements with Geely HK and Etika, allowing them to require the company to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd at a set price, contingent on the sale of over 5,000 vehicles in 2024. The exercise of these options, which can occur between April and June 2025, poses a risk of significant financial obligations that could negatively impact liquidity and overall financial health. Future capital raising efforts, whether through equity or debt, may lead to shareholder dilution or increased financial obligations, further complicating operational flexibility and dividend distributions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our business plans require a significant amount of capital. In addition, our future capital needs may require us to obtain additional equity or debt financing that may dilute our shareholders or introduce covenants that may restrict our operations or our ability to pay dividends. 
summary: The company is preparing for significant capital needs to support its research and development, production expansion, and the establishment of a sales and service network. As operations grow, ongoing capital may be required to maintain facilities, with costs potentially surpassing current estimates. However, uncertainty surrounds future consumer demand and capital requirements due to the company's limited operating history, which could lead to actual needs diverging from projections. To address these expenditures, the company may need to seek equity or debt financing, though there are risks associated with the availability and terms of such funding. Failure to secure sufficient capital could adversely affect the company's operations, financial condition, and growth prospects, potentially necessitating cost-cutting measures or restructuring.

Additionally, the company has entered into put option agreements with Geely HK and Etika, allowing them to require the company to buy back their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, contingent on the sale of over 5,000 vehicles in 2024. The exercise of these options could create significant financial obligations, impacting liquidity and overall financial health.

To meet future capital needs, the company may have to issue more equity or debt, which could dilute existing shareholders or increase financial burdens. Furthermore, the company faces risks related to its suppliers' adherence to ethical practices and compliance with laws, which could damage its brand reputation and lead to negative publicity. This situation raises concerns about potential operational disruptions and increased costs if non-compliance necessitates finding alternative suppliers. Overall, these factors could significantly impact the company's brand image, demand for its electric vehicles, and long-term business viability.

As Lotus Technology ramps up its production capacity and operations, the company may also require significant capital to maintain its property, plant, and equipment, and such costs may be greater than what the company currently anticipates. Lotus Technology expects that its level of capital expenditures will be significantly affected by consumer demand for its products and services. The fact that Lotus Technology has a limited operating history means the company has limited historical data on the demand for its products and services. As a result, Lotus Technology's future capital requirements may be uncertain, and actual capital requirements may be significantly different from what the company currently anticipates. These factors may make the timing, amount, terms, and conditions of such financing unattractive or unavailable to Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our business plans require a significant amount of capital. In addition, our future capital needs may require us to obtain additional equity or debt financing that may dilute our shareholders or introduce covenants that may restrict our operations or our ability to pay dividends. 
summary: The company is facing significant capital needs to support its growth initiatives, including research and development, production expansion, and the establishment of a sales and service network. As operations scale, maintenance costs for property and equipment may exceed expectations, and capital expenditures will largely depend on unpredictable consumer demand due to the company's limited operating history. This uncertainty in future capital requirements could lead to discrepancies from current projections, necessitating potential equity or debt financing, which may be challenging to secure on favorable terms. Failure to obtain adequate funding could hinder business operations, forcing the company to reduce spending, delay investments, or restructure significantly. Additionally, concerns about managing existing debts could further strain resources, risking the cessation of activities. The company has also entered into put option agreements with Geely International and Etika, allowing them to require the company to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies at a set price, contingent on vehicle sales in 2024. The exercise of these options could impose substantial financial obligations, impacting liquidity and overall financial health. To address future capital needs, the company may need to issue more equity or debt, which could dilute shareholder ownership or increase debt service obligations, potentially limiting operational flexibility and dividend payments.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In the period leading up to the Closing of the Business Combination, events may occur that, pursuant to the Merger Agreement, would require LCAA to agree to amend the Merger Agreement, to consent to certain actions taken by Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) or to waive rights that LCAA is entitled to under the Merger Agreement. Such events could arise because of the changes in the course of LTC’s business, a request by LTC to undertake actions that would otherwise be prohibited by the terms of the Merger Agreement or the occurrence of other events that would have a material adverse effect on LTC’s business or could entitle LCAA to terminate the Merger Agreement. In any of such circumstances, it would be at LCAA’s discretion, acting through the LCAA Board, to grant its consent or waive those rights; provided that under the terms of the Merger Agreement, such consent or waiver in certain cases is not to be unreasonably withheld. The existence of financial and personal interests of one or more of the directors may result in conflicts of interests on the part of such director(s) between what the directors of LCAA may believe is the best for LCAA and what the directors may believe is the best for themselves in determining whether or not to take the requested action.

metadata.title_summary:
title: The exercise of LCAA directors’ discretion in agreeing to changes or waivers in the terms of the Business Combination may result in a conflict of interests when determining whether such changes to the terms of the Business Combination or waivers of conditions are appropriate and in LCAA’s best interest. 
summary: As LCAA approaches the Closing of the Business Combination, various circumstances may require amendments to the Merger Agreement, consent to LTC's requests, or waivers of certain rights. These situations could arise from changes in LTC's operations or other factors that might adversely affect its business, potentially leading LCAA to consider terminating the agreement. The Board of LCAA has the authority to grant such consents or waivers, provided they are not unreasonably withheld. However, potential conflicts of interest among directors, stemming from their personal and financial stakes, could influence their decision-making. The proxy statement/prospectus details the considerations for LCAA's directors and officers regarding the Business Combination and emphasizes that, despite differing personal views, no changes or waivers are expected post-shareholder approval. Should any significant alterations to the transaction terms occur that could impact shareholders, LCAA pledges to issue a revised proxy statement/prospectus and seek further shareholder input. Furthermore, under Cayman Islands law, LCAA's directors are obligated to prioritize the company's best interests in their decisions.
